compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: META EVIDENCE from the single-pass G3/G4 review of THIS epic (run as the merge+pack extreme — one subagent over all 6 children instead of the 2N fan-out): evaluating all children TOGETHER IMPROVED cross-child (G4) recall — it surfaced an inter-child gap (S2's unverified prompt relocation) that a one-child-at-a-time fan-out would have MISSED (each child reads fine alone; the gap lives between S2 and S6). This validates the design hypothesis that G3/G4 (cross-child criteria) benefit from seeing children together. CAVEAT: attention skewed to the first/most-annotated children; TAIL children got less granular review (recency/salience bias). => batching preserves/improves cross-child fidelity but risks per-child DEPTH on tail children; the REQUIRED structured per-child output guardrail (already in S5) is ESSENTIAL and must also apply to S4. S6's eval should specifically check TAIL-child recall.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
[G3 child-coverage audit — parent c81c plan-review fan-out epic; this child S4 (merge G3+G4 into one call per bin) reviewed WHOLE against the full sibling roster]

4-bucket audit of the parent's acceptance/success criteria, mapping S4's contribution and cross-checking every absence against the roster (S1 0250, S2 c6e5, S3 ba7e, S5 1762, S6 da34):

- "materially lower token cost (cache reads non-zero) / completes in minutes" (AC2 / Success) — PARTIALLY covered here: S4 halves container calls 2N->N, a direct token-and-call reduction (AC: "container call count halves (2N -> N)"). Wall-clock parallelization is delegated to sibling S3 (ba7e), the cache cost lever to S1 (0250). The 2N->N projection is grounded — S4's remediation correctly targets the live `container_floor_usd = len(container) * n_children * COST_AGENT_USD` at sizing.py:232. Covered across roster.
- "findings within offline-eval tolerance / no fidelity regression" (AC4 / Success) — PARTIALLY covered here: S4 carries the MERGE-SPECIFIC fidelity obligation ("offline eval shows no accuracy regression vs separate G3/G4 calls" + "S6 harness shows merged-call findings within tolerance"), correctly gated on the S6 semantic harness (DEPENDS ON S6, declared via the da34->98c6 blocks link). The merge's distinct attribution risk (model-self-reported G3-vs-G4, validate-and-drop mirroring passes.py:222-224 — confirmed: the existing container path hardcodes `[cid]` at passes.py:277, which the merge necessarily replaces) is covered by S4's own ACs. Covered.
- "caching provider-safe: openai/gemini succeed unchanged" (AC3) — STRUCTURAL w.r.t. S4 (no caching surface; owned by S1/S2). Covered by roster.
- "make lint + full suite pass" (AC5) — per-child portion covered here ("`make lint` + tests pass").
- "caching across plan-review AND all workflow agent steps" (Success) — outside S4 scope (S1 runner seam + S2 engine-wide). Covered by roster.
- "all six child stories (S1-S6) closed" (AC1) — aggregate/structural; not a single-child mapping.

ANTI-FP cross-check on the one plausible absence: the per-child DEPTH / tail-child guardrail that batching introduces (the "REQUIRED structured per-child output guardrail" the epic meta-review flags as ESSENTIAL and "must also apply to S4") is a CONSISTENCY/completeness concern across S4/S5/S6, not an uncovered PARENT acceptance/success criterion — and the roster already carries it (S5 owns the guardrail, S6's eval is tasked to check tail-child recall). It is therefore not a G3 absence finding for this pairing.

Result: G3 CLEAN — no findings. S4 is a coherent contributor to the coverage map; no parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered by the union of children, and S4 introduces no coverage gap that no sibling fills.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
CONTAINER review (G4 — child consistency), parent c81c (plan-review fan-out optimization). Checked the 7 cross-child interaction modes for THIS child (S4, merge G3+G4) against the whole sibling roster (S1 0250, S2 c6e5, S3 ba7e, S5 1762, S6 da34) and the parent. ONE G4 FINDING (scope-overlap / conflicting-assumption: the required per-child output guardrail is owned by S5 but load-bearing for S4 and absent from S4's spec). Affirmations and the finding below.

AFFIRMATIONS (modes that read CLEAN under ANTI-FP):

- Implicit shared state: `sizing.py` container-cost projection (`len(cont)*n_children*COST_AGENT`, sizing.py:206) is touched by THREE siblings in sequence — S3 (cost-model fix + "container never shed"), S4 (this child: 2N->N), S5 (<N after packing). Real shared state, but EXPLICITLY ordering-resolved by the parent's Sequencing note ("Land S3 -> S4 -> S5 STRICTLY in DAG order: S4/S5's sizing.py container-cost projections are only correct AFTER S3's 'container never shed' + cost-model fix lands") and the DAG (S3->S4->S5). S4's own remediation AC ("sizing.py container-cost projection reflects N calls; shedding stays calibrated") correctly layers on S3's fix. No contradiction.

- Dependency gap: NONE. The DAG S3->S4 and S6->S4 are both modeled (S3 ba7e `blocks` 98c6; S6 da34 `blocks` 98c6), and S4 `blocks` S5 (1762) — matching the parent DAG `S1->{S2,S3}; S3->S4; S6->{S4,S5}; S4->S5`. S4's remediation correctly re-points the fidelity gate from "vague offline eval" to the S6 semantic harness (DEPENDS ON S6). Incoming deps consistent.

- Conflicting assumptions (cost/shedding): S4's "halve container calls" premise is consistent with S3's de-risk-D finding (halving container cost pushes the shed threshold past N=40 but S3's stronger "never shed" rule is the robust fix) — S4 is positioned as a major MITIGATION, not a competing fix. No conflict.

- Ordering violation: NONE intrinsic — S4 is the middle node of the strict S3->S4->S5 chain; it takes the sizing projection from 2N to N, S5 then takes it N->{<N}. Correctly sequenced.

- Consumer impact: S4 restructures the per-call criterion vocabulary (1 criterion -> 2) over the call structure S3 produces (new pool + warm-then-fan-out); S5 consumes S4's merged-call shape. Producer->consumer is DAG-ordered. The attribution remediation (validate `criteria` against {G3,G4}, drop out-of-set, preserve `_container_child`, mirror passes.py:222-224) correctly protects the coach/sidecar downstream consumers of the finding->criterion mapping. Consistent.

- Residual references: S4's body still says "Gate behind an OFFLINE EVAL"/"offline eval shows no accuracy regression" while the remediation supersedes this with "Fidelity gate = the S6 semantic eval harness (DEPENDS ON S6), not a vague 'offline eval'." This is a stale label within S4, superseded in-ticket by its own remediation — an intra-child editorial residue, not a cross-child contradiction (S6's own ticket carries the matching semantic-harness contract). Below the cross-child/high-confidence threshold; recorded for editorial cleanup only, NOT a G4 finding.

G4 FINDING:

- finding: The "REQUIRED structured per-child output guardrail" is owned by sibling S5 (1762: "Structured per-child sections in the prompt + REQUIRED per-child output so per-child attention isn't lost") but is load-bearing for S4 and ABSENT from S4's spec — a scope/ownership split that leaves S4 without the guardrail it depends on. The shared meta-evidence comment (carried on S4/S5/S6, ts 1782572460/463/466) states the batching tail-child recency/salience bias "risks per-child DEPTH on tail children" and that the guardrail "(already in S5) is ESSENTIAL and must ALSO apply to S4." S4 already batches G3+G4 over a (child/bin) and is the FIRST batching child in the DAG (S4->S5), so the very fidelity hazard the guardrail mitigates is present at S4, yet S4's What/AC mention no per-child structured-output requirement. Without it, S4's own AC ("findings correctly attributed to G3 vs G4"; "no accuracy regression within tolerance") can pass on aggregate counts while silently under-reviewing a tail child — a cross-child consistency gap between S4 and S5 (and an unmet obligation flagged by the shared S6 note).
  criteria: [G4]
  location: child 98c6 ("## What" / "## Acceptance Criteria"); cross-ref sibling 1762 S5 "## What" (REQUIRED per-child output) + shared comment ts 1782572460471843001
  evidence:
    - "S5 (1762) body: 'Structured per-child sections in the prompt + REQUIRED per-child output so per-child attention isn't lost' — the guardrail lives in S5 only."
    - "Shared meta-comment on S4/S5/S6: 'the REQUIRED structured per-child output guardrail (already in S5) is ESSENTIAL and must also apply to S4. S6's eval should specifically check TAIL-child recall.'"
    - "S4 '## What' specifies the merged dual-rubric call + attribution but no per-child/bin structured-output completeness requirement; S4 'Why' already frames the call as 'per (child/bin)', i.e. it can batch >1 child, so the tail-recall hazard applies at S4."
    - "Mode = scope overlap + conflicting assumption: the guardrail's ownership sits in S5 while the dependency on it arises one step earlier in S4 (S4->S5), so under strict DAG ordering S4 lands WITHOUT it."
  scenarios:
    - "S4 lands (S5 not yet started). A merged G3+G4 call over a 2-child bin attends mostly to the head child; the tail child gets a shallow pass. Aggregate G3/G4 finding counts match the separate-call baseline within tolerance, so S4's AC and the S6 recall metric (if it does not check per-child/tail recall) both pass — but the tail child's child-coverage/consistency was effectively unreviewed. Fidelity AC silently violated."
  impact: "S4 can satisfy its tolerance/attribution AC while regressing per-child (tail) review depth, because the per-child completeness guardrail that prevents this is scoped to the LATER sibling S5. The parent's fidelity AC ('no fidelity regression vs current per-child behavior') is at risk for the first batching child."
  checklist_item: "Check the 7 cross-child modes — scope overlap / conflicting assumptions (the per-child guardrail S4 depends on is owned by a later sibling and absent from S4)."
  suggested_fix: "Add to S4's '## What' and AC a REQUIRED structured per-child/per-bin output guarantee (each child id in the bin returns a finding list OR an explicit 'clean' marker), mirroring S5's guardrail, so the merged call cannot silently omit a tail child; and have the S6 fidelity gate (S4's declared gate) explicitly check TAIL-child recall, per the shared meta-comment. Either pull the guardrail forward into S4 or make S4's fidelity AC depend on S5's guardrail landing first."

Note (NOT a G4 finding, recorded for editorial cleanup): S4's body 'OFFLINE EVAL' language is superseded by its own remediation ('Fidelity gate = the S6 semantic eval harness') — an intra-child stale label, consistent across the roster (S6 carries the matching semantic-harness contract). Reads benignly under ANTI-FP.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
CONTAINER review (G4 — child consistency), parent c81c (plan-review fan-out optimization). Walked the 7 cross-child interaction modes for THIS child (S4, merge G3+G4 into one call per bin) WHOLE against the complete sibling roster (S1 0250, S2 c6e5, S3 ba7e, S5 1762, S6 da34) and the parent. ONE G4 FINDING (scope-overlap + conflicting-assumption: the per-child output guardrail S4 depends on is owned by the LATER sibling S5 and is absent from S4). Corroborates the prior container-review comment (ts 1782578778460634001).

AFFIRMATIONS (modes CLEAN under ANTI-FP):
- Implicit shared state: sizing.py container-cost projection (len(cont)*n_children*COST_AGENT, sizing.py:206/232) is touched by S3 (cost-model fix + container-never-shed), S4 (this child: 2N->N), S5 (<N after packing). Real shared state, but EXPLICITLY ordering-resolved by the parent Sequencing note ("Land S3 -> S4 -> S5 STRICTLY in DAG order") and the DAG S3->S4->S5. S4's remediation AC ("sizing.py projection reflects N calls; shedding stays calibrated") layers correctly on S3. No contradiction.
- Dependency gap: NONE. S3 (ba7e) blocks 98c6; S6 (da34) blocks 98c6; S4 blocks S5 (1762) — matching parent DAG S1->{S2,S3}; S3->S4; S6->{S4,S5}; S4->S5. S4's remediation re-points the fidelity gate to the S6 semantic harness (DEPENDS ON S6), correctly declared.
- Conflicting assumptions (cost/shedding): S4's "halve container calls" is consistent with S3's de-risk-D (halving pushes shed threshold past N=40; S3's "never shed" is the robust fix). S4 is positioned as a MITIGATION, not a competing fix. No conflict.
- Ordering violation: NONE intrinsic — S4 is the middle node of the strict S3->S4->S5 chain; it takes the projection 2N->N, S5 then N->{<N}.
- Consumer impact: S4 restructures the per-call criterion vocabulary (1->2) over the call structure S3 produces; S5 consumes S4's merged-call shape. Producer->consumer is DAG-ordered. The attribution remediation (validate criteria against {G3,G4}, drop out-of-set, preserve _container_child, mirror passes.py:222-224) correctly protects the coach/sidecar downstream consumers of the finding->criterion mapping. Consistent.
- Residual references: S4's body still says "Gate behind an OFFLINE EVAL" while its own remediation supersedes this with "Fidelity gate = the S6 semantic eval harness (DEPENDS ON S6), not a vague 'offline eval'." This is an intra-child stale label superseded in-ticket by its own remediation, with S6's own ticket carrying the matching semantic-harness contract — reads benignly under ANTI-FP. Recorded for editorial cleanup only, NOT a G4 finding.

G4 FINDING:
- finding: The "REQUIRED structured per-child output guardrail" is owned by sibling S5 (1762: "Structured per-child sections in the prompt + REQUIRED per-child output so per-child attention isn't lost") but is load-bearing for S4 and ABSENT from S4's spec. S4's own "Why" frames the merged call as "per (child/bin)" — i.e. it can batch >1 child — and S4 is the FIRST batching child in the strict S4->S5 chain. So the very batching tail-recall hazard the guardrail mitigates is present AT S4, yet S4's What/AC carry no per-child structured-output completeness requirement. The shared meta-evidence comment carried on S4/S5/S6 (ts 1782572460/463/466) states the batching recency/salience bias "risks per-child DEPTH on tail children" and that the guardrail "(already in S5) is ESSENTIAL and must ALSO apply to S4," and that S6's eval "should specifically check TAIL-child recall." Under strict DAG ordering, S4 lands WITHOUT the guardrail S5 owns — a scope/ownership split and a conflicting assumption (S4 assumes per-child fidelity is preserved; the mechanism that preserves it lives one step later).
  criteria: [G4]
  location: child 98c6 "## What" / "## Acceptance Criteria"; cross-ref sibling 1762 (S5) "## What" (REQUIRED per-child output) + shared meta-comment ts 1782572460471843001
  evidence:
    - "S5 (1762) body: 'Structured per-child sections in the prompt + REQUIRED per-child output so per-child attention isn't lost' — the guardrail is scoped to S5 only."
    - "Shared meta-comment on S4/S5/S6: 'the REQUIRED structured per-child output guardrail (already in S5) is ESSENTIAL and must also apply to S4. S6's eval should specifically check TAIL-child recall.'"
    - "S4 '## What' specifies the merged dual-rubric call + attribution but no per-child/bin structured-output completeness requirement; S4 '## Why' frames the call as 'per (child/bin)', so it can batch >1 child and the tail-recall hazard applies at S4."
    - "Mode = scope overlap + conflicting assumption: the guardrail's ownership sits in S5 while the dependency on it arises one step earlier in S4 (S4->S5), so under the parent's strict DAG ordering S4 lands WITHOUT it."
  scenarios:
    - "S4 lands (S5 not yet started). A merged G3+G4 call over a >1-child bin attends mostly to the head child; the tail child gets a shallow pass. Aggregate G3/G4 finding counts match the separate-call baseline within tolerance, so S4's AC ('correctly attributed'; 'no accuracy regression within tolerance') and the S6 recall metric (if it does not check per-child/tail recall) both pass — but the tail child's child-coverage/consistency was effectively unreviewed; the parent fidelity AC is silently violated for the first batching child."
  impact: "S4 can satisfy its tolerance/attribution AC while regressing per-child (tail) review depth, because the per-child completeness guardrail that prevents this is scoped to the LATER sibling S5. The parent's fidelity AC ('no fidelity regression vs the current per-child behavior') is at risk for the first batching child in the DAG."
  checklist_item: "Check the 7 cross-child modes — scope overlap / conflicting assumptions (the per-child guardrail S4 depends on is owned by a later sibling and absent from S4)."
  suggested_fix: "Add to S4's '## What' and AC a REQUIRED structured per-child/per-bin output guarantee (each child id in the bin returns a finding list OR an explicit 'clean' marker), mirroring S5's guardrail, so the merged call cannot silently omit a tail child; and have S4's declared S6 fidelity gate explicitly check TAIL-child recall, per the shared meta-comment. Alternatively, pull S5's guardrail forward into S4, or make S4's fidelity AC depend on S5's guardrail landing first."

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: FORCE_CLAIM: plan-review gate bypassed by user approval — no plan-review attestation was verified. Reason: "Implemented + independent subagent code review PASS + full suite green (2752 passed); gate returns unsigned INDETERMINATE doc-nits, not a BLOCK.".

compiled_state.description:
# Merge G3+G4 into one container call per bin (2N -> N)

## Why
G3 (child-coverage) and G4 (child-consistency) share the identical (parent, child, roster) context and are
disjoint audits the container prompt already describes together. Evaluating both in one call halves container
calls (2N -> N).

## What
- Evaluate G3 AND G4 in ONE call per (child/bin): present both rubrics over the shared context (`passes.py` +
  `plan_review_container.md`). Keep per-criterion attribution in the output (findings tagged G3 vs G4).
- Gate behind an OFFLINE EVAL confirming combined-call findings match separate-call findings within tolerance
  (two rubrics in one turn must not dilute either).

## Scope
- In: the merged G3+G4 container call + attribution + the offline eval.
- Out: parallelization (S3, dependency); bin-packing (S5).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] G3 and G4 are evaluated in one call per bin; findings are correctly attributed to G3 vs G4.
- [ ] An offline eval shows no accuracy regression vs separate G3/G4 calls (within tolerance).
- [ ] Container call count halves (2N -> N) for an epic; `make lint` + tests pass.

## Remediation (opus review — MAJOR 5/7, BLOCKER 3)
- Attribution flips from code-assigned (`passes.py:276` hardcodes `[cid]`) to model-self-reported. VALIDATE the
  returned `criteria` against {G3,G4} and DROP out-of-set tags (mirror passes.py:222-224); preserve the
  `_container_child` provenance (passes.py:288).
- Update the sizing.py container-cost projection from `2N` to `N` calls (it currently hardcodes 2*children).
- Fidelity gate = the S6 semantic eval harness (DEPENDS ON S6), not a vague "offline eval".
- [ ] Merged-call findings with `criteria` not in {G3,G4} are dropped (not mis-tagged); `_container_child` preserved.
- [ ] sizing.py container-cost projection reflects N calls (post-merge); shedding stays calibrated.
- [ ] The S6 fidelity harness shows merged-call findings within tolerance of separate G3/G4 calls.

## CI/eval posture (S6)
- The S6 semantic fidelity eval is a HUMAN-REVIEWED development gate run before merge (live, opt-in), NOT a required/blocking CI check (non-deterministic). The offline eval-spec discipline check still runs on every PR.

file_impact.reason: single merged G3+G4 call + criteria validation against {G3,G4}

compiled_state.title: Merge G3+G4 into one container call per bin (2N -> N)
